sources.publisher: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
sources.quote: 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity a week ... or 75 minutes of vigorous-intensity ... and 2 days of muscle-strengthening activity each week.

factsExtracted.value: CDC physical activity guidelines for adults
Federal guideline-based weekly activity plan combining moderate-intensity aerobic activity with muscle-strengthening activity targeting all major muscle groups.
150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity a week, or 75 minutes of vigorous-intensity activity, or an equivalent combination of both
Aerobic activity can be distributed as 30 minutes a day, 5 days a week; muscle-strengthening activity at least 2 days a week
Muscle-strengthening activity should target all major muscle groups: legs, hips, back, abdomen, chest, shoulders, and arms
Moderate-intensity examples include brisk walking; vigorous-intensity examples include jogging or running
Aerobic minutes can be broken up into smaller chunks of time across the week
U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
